taxonomy_v3: Engineering and Development, Data Analysis, Data Scientist
core_skills: SQL, SQL query optimization, Relational databases, Data analysis, Data profiling
skill_match_scores: SQL:1.0, SQL query optimization:1.0, Relational databases:1.0, Data analysis:1.0, Data profiling:1.0
must_have_quals: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Information Systems, or related degree, 5+ years of experience writing complex SQL queries including nested joins, temporary tables, aggregations, subqueries, window functions, and CTEs, 5+ years of experience with relational databases (PostgreSQL, SQL Server, MySQL) and SQL query performance optimization, Experience with data profiling, data quality assessment, data validation techniques, and finding insights in large, complex data sets, Strong analytical skills including data mining and evaluation, with demonstrated ability to perform code/query reviews in Agile/Scrum environments, Proficient with multiple technologies including SQL databases, data analysis tools, BI platforms, and scripting languages, Expert-level SQL skills with deep understanding of complex queries, query optimization, and performance tuning, Strong analytical skills including data mining, evaluation, analysis, and visualization, Identifies, implements, and applies best practices for data analysis and quality; is the 'go to' person on the team, Ability to handle multiple projects and possesses a proven track record of high-quality deliverables
preferred_quals: Hands-on experience with Snowflake data warehouse and Sigma Computing for building analytics reports and dashboards, Familiarity with healthcare data rules and standards (HIPAA, HL7) or healthcare supply chain/IT systems
core_responsibilities: Write and optimize complex SQL queries including nested joins, temp tables, aggregations, window functions, and CTEs, Identify patterns and relationships to evaluate, maintain, and enrich data quality across multiple systems and data sources, Perform root cause analysis (RCA) and impact analysis to identify data quality issues and report findings to stakeholders, Develop flexible, reusable, and efficient SQL code with minimal supervision while adhering to best practices, Create and maintain analytics reports and dashboards for business stakeholders using modern BI tools, Conduct data profiling and data quality validation to ensure high-quality data for downstream consumers, Perform code reviews on SQL and data scripts, providing constructive feedback to ensure quality, Collaborate with cross-functional teams including data engineering, product, and analytics teams in an Agile/Scrum environment
job_summary: Global Healthcare Exchange (GHX) is a healthcare business and data automation company that empowers healthcare organizations to enhance patient care and maximize savings through advanced technology solutions. They are seeking a Technical Data Analyst III to focus on data analysis, reporting, and data quality, ensuring the integrity of content data through complex SQL development and analytics reporting.
